metadata.description: Name of the environment (used for resource naming)
Principal ID of the deploying user for role assignments
Publisher email for the AI Gateway (APIM)
Path prefix for the agent API on the gateway (set during Foundry portal registration)
Location for the Static Web App (must be one of: centralus, eastus2, westus2, westeurope, eastasia)
Name of the Log Analytics workspace

parameters.name: [if(not(empty(parameters('appInsightsName'))), createObject('value', parameters('appInsightsName')), createObject('value', format('{0}{1}', variables('abbrs').insightsComponents, variables('resourceToken'))))]

metadata.description: Name of the Application Insights resource

parameters.name: [if(not(empty(parameters('keyVaultName'))), createObject('value', parameters('keyVaultName')), createObject('value', format('{0}{1}', variables('abbrs').keyVaultVaults, variables('resourceToken'))))]

metadata.description: VNet ID for DNS zone link
Name of the Cosmos DB account
VNet ID for DNS zone link
Key Vault name for storing connection info

Public network access for the Cosmos DB account.
Defaults to 'Enabled' because Foundry hosted agents run in a Microsoft-managed
network OUTSIDE the customer VNet and therefore cannot reach the private
endpoint — with 'Disabled' the agent's conversation-persistence calls fail at
the network layer (before RBAC is even evaluated). The data plane stays locked
down: local (key) auth is disabled, so only Microsoft Entra ID identities with
an explicit Cosmos data-plane role can connect. Set to 'Disabled' only if you
front the agent with VNet injection / a managed private endpoint.

parameters.name: [if(not(empty(parameters('aiSearchName'))), createObject('value', parameters('aiSearchName')), createObject('value', format('{0}{1}', variables('abbrs').searchSearchServices, variables('resourceToken'))))]

metadata.description: Name of the AI Search service
VNet ID for DNS zone link

Public network access for the AI Search service.
Defaults to 'enabled' for the same reason as Cosmos: Foundry hosted agents run
in a Microsoft-managed network outside the customer VNet and cannot reach the
private endpoint, so the rag-search skill (which connects to the Search data
plane with DefaultAzureCredential) fails at the network layer when this is
'disabled'. Access stays gated by Microsoft Entra ID RBAC via the role
assignments. Set to 'disabled' only with VNet injection / a managed private
endpoint in front of the agent.
Name of the Microsoft Foundry resource
Name of the GPT model deployment
Deployment SKU capacity (thousands of tokens per minute)

parameters.name: [if(not(empty(parameters('bingSearchName'))), createObject('value', parameters('bingSearchName')), createObject('value', format('{0}{1}', variables('abbrs').bingSearchAccounts, variables('resourceToken'))))]

metadata.description: Name of the Bing Grounding resource
Key Vault name to store the API key

parameters.name: [if(not(empty(parameters('storageAccountName'))), createObject('value', parameters('storageAccountName')), createObject('value', format('{0}{1}', variables('abbrs').storageAccounts, variables('resourceToken'))))]

metadata.description: Name of the blob container for skills
Name of the Container Apps environment

parameters.name: [if(not(empty(parameters('agentServiceName'))), createObject('value', parameters('agentServiceName')), createObject('value', format('{0}agent-{1}', variables('abbrs').appContainerApps, variables('resourceToken'))))]

metadata.description: App Insights resource ID (for resource-scoped KQL queries from the Traces panel)
Hosted agent name deployed in Foundry
Azure Blob Storage endpoint for skills
Static Web App URL for CORS (e.g. https://xxx.azurestaticapps.net)
Name of the API Management service

parameters.name: [if(not(empty(parameters('staticWebAppName'))), createObject('value', parameters('staticWebAppName')), createObject('value', format('{0}{1}', variables('abbrs').webStaticSites, variables('resourceToken'))))]

metadata.description: Name of the Static Web App
Display name for the Entra application
Unique name for the Entra application
True for the OBO server app; false for the SPA client app
Value of the delegated scope exposed by the server app

entraAppScopeDisplayName.defaultValue: Access the OBO MCP server as the signed-in user

metadata.description: Display name of the delegated scope

entraAppScopeDescription.defaultValue: Allows the agent to call Microsoft Graph on behalf of the signed-in user.

metadata.description: App id of the SPA client to pre-authorize on the server app (server only)
Redirect URIs for the SPA client app (client only)
Object (principal) id of the container app user-assigned managed identity. Required when isServer is true — it is the FIC subject.
FIC token-exchange audience. Public cloud: api://AzureADTokenExchange.
Optional Service Management Reference GUID for the Entra application.

properties.api: [if(parameters('isServer'), createObject('oauth2PermissionScopes', createArray(createObject('id', variables('scopeId'), 'type', 'User', 'adminConsentDescription', parameters('entraAppScopeDescription'), 'adminConsentDisplayName', parameters('entraAppScopeDisplayName'), 'userConsentDescription', parameters('entraAppScopeDescription'), 'userConsentDisplayName', parameters('entraAppScopeDisplayName'), 'value', parameters('entraAppScopeValue'), 'isEnabled', true())), 'preAuthorizedApplications', createArray(createObject('appId', parameters('knownClientAppId'), 'delegatedPermissionIds', createArray(variables('scopeId'))), createObject('appId', variables('vsCodeClientAppId'), 'delegatedPermissionIds', createArray(variables('scopeId')))), 'requestedAccessTokenVersion', 2), null())]

metadata.description: Resource id of the OBO user-assigned managed identity
Client id of the OBO user-assigned managed identity (selects the MI for the token-exchange assertion)
Principal (object) id of the OBO user-assigned managed identity (for the AcrPull role)
Tenant id that issues + validates user tokens
appId of the OBO server app registration (OBO client_id + expected audience)
Downstream Microsoft Graph delegated scope(s), space-separated
Space-separated client app ids allowed to call this server (azp/appid allow-list)
Principal ID of the agent service managed identity
Microsoft Foundry system-assigned principal ID (compile-time output from the ai-services module)

Microsoft Foundry PROJECT system-assigned principal ID. Per foundry-hosted-agents skill, this is the MI that actually pulls the hosted-agent container image from ACR — granting AcrPull only to the account MI is insufficient and causes ImageError at first invoke.
Storage Account name for skills blob storage
Application Insights resource name (for Monitoring Reader RBAC)
Container Registry name (for AcrPull grant to the Foundry hosted-agent MI)
